response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack software engineer with over 15 years of experience in web, mobile, and backend development, but his background has virtually no overlap with the core requirements of this Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep hardware expertise — SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, and deep neural network inference optimization — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His AI-related work at Google is limited to software tooling (MCP frameworks, agentic workflows), not hardware acceleration or DL workload analysis. His low-latency systems work at Sociomantic is the closest adjacent signal, but it is software-level performance optimization, not hardware-software co-design.
strengths: 15+ years of progressive software engineering experience demonstrating strong technical growth and adaptability across diverse stacks and domains
Low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic (async I/O, high-throughput microservices) shows some performance engineering sensibility
Current role at Google involving AI-agent infrastructure provides loose thematic proximity to the AI industry
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or hardware-software co-design — the core technical requirements of the role
No background in deep neural network training or inference optimization, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or AI accelerator workloads
Educational background (two Bachelor's degrees, no Master's or PhD) combined with a purely software-focused career trajectory does not meet the depth expected at Principal/Senior IC hardware level
